quizQuestion:
彼は漢字{___}、ひらがなも書けない。
(He can't write even hiragana, let alone kanji.)
explanation: はおろか (let alone) — 彼は漢字はおろか、ひらがなも書けない。Used to say that if even the lesser thing is impossible, the greater is certainly so.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その会社は利益{___}、毎月赤字が続いている。
(That company, let alone profits, is running deficits every month.)
explanation: はおろか (let alone) — その会社は利益はおろか、毎月赤字が続いている。Emphasizes that even the minimal expectation is not met.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
忙しくて食事{___}、水も飲む暇がない。
(I'm so busy that I have no time to drink even water, let alone eat meals.)
explanation: はおろか (let alone) — 食事はおろか、水も飲む暇がない。The lesser item follows with も or すら.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は敬語{___}、普通の丁寧語も使えない。
(She can't even use polite language, let alone honorific speech.)
explanation: はおろか (let alone) — 敬語はおろか、普通の丁寧語も使えない。Scales from harder to easier, both unachieved.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の技術{___}、その熱意にも感心した。
(Not only his skill, but I was also impressed by his passion.)
explanation: もさることながら (not only A but also B) — 技術もさることながら、熱意にも感心した。A is acknowledged as given; B is equally or more notable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
価格{___}、品質の高さが評判を呼んでいる。
(Not only the price, but the high quality has also earned it a good reputation.)
explanation: もさることながら (not only A but B) — 価格もさることながら、品質の高さが評判を呼んでいる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
外観の美しさ{___}、内装のデザインも素晴らしい。
(Not only the beautiful exterior, but the interior design is also wonderful.)
explanation: もさることながら (not only A but B) — 外観の美しさもさることながら、内装のデザインも素晴らしい。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女の歌唱力{___}、ステージ上の存在感が際立っている。
(Not only her singing ability, but her stage presence is also outstanding.)
explanation: もさることながら (not only A but B) — 歌唱力もさることながら、存在感が際立っている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
周囲の心配{___}、彼は危険な山に一人で登った。
(Ignoring the worries of those around him, he climbed the dangerous mountain alone.)
explanation: をよそに (ignoring/indifferent to) — 周囲の心配をよそに、彼は登った。Subject acts regardless of others' concerns.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
国民の反対{___}、政府はその法案を強行採決した。
(Ignoring the public opposition, the government pushed through the bill.)
explanation: をよそに (ignoring) — 国民の反対をよそに、強行採決した。Often used with negative connotation of disregarding others.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
親の期待{___}、息子は音楽の道を選んだ。
(Ignoring his parents' expectations, the son chose a path in music.)
explanation: をよそに (ignoring) — 親の期待をよそに、音楽の道を選んだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
周りの喧騒{___}、彼女は静かに本を読み続けた。
(Oblivious to the commotion around her, she continued reading quietly.)
explanation: をよそに (ignoring/oblivious to) — 周りの喧騒をよそに、静かに本を読み続けた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}、明日までに返事をしなければならない。
(In any case, I have to give a reply by tomorrow.)
explanation: いずれにしても (in any case/either way) — いずれにしても、明日までに返事をしなければならない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
賛成でも反対でも、{___}、会議に出席することが必要だ。
(Whether you agree or disagree, in any case you need to attend the meeting.)
explanation: いずれにしても (in any case) — 賛成でも反対でも、いずれにしても出席が必要だ。Covers all possible outcomes.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
成功しても失敗しても、{___}、全力を尽くすことが大切だ。
(Whether you succeed or fail, in any case it is important to give your all.)
explanation: いずれにしても (in any case) — 成功しても失敗しても、いずれにしても全力を尽くすことが大切だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
行くかどうかはまだわからないが、{___}、準備だけはしておこう。
(I still don't know whether I'll go, but in any case let me at least prepare.)
explanation: いずれにしても (in any case) — いずれにしても、準備だけはしておこう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の作品はどれも素晴らしいが、{___}この絵が気に入っている。
(All of his works are wonderful, but I especially like this painting.)
explanation: とりわけ (especially/particularly) — とりわけこの絵が気に入っている。Selects one from a group as most notable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今年の夏は暑かったが、{___}8月が耐えがたいほどだった。
(This summer was hot, but August in particular was unbearable.)
explanation: とりわけ (especially) — とりわけ8月が耐えがたいほどだった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供たちは外遊びが好きだが、{___}サッカーに夢中だ。
(The children like playing outside, but they are especially crazy about soccer.)
explanation: とりわけ (especially) — とりわけサッカーに夢中だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女はスポーツが得意だが、{___}水泳では全国大会に出場した。
(She is good at sports, but she especially competed in the national tournament in swimming.)
explanation: とりわけ (especially) — とりわけ水泳では全国大会に出場した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の提案は{___}否決された。
(All of his proposals were entirely rejected.)
explanation: ことごとく (entirely/all without exception) — 彼の提案はことごとく否決された。Emphatic 'all' with negative outcome common.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
嵐は村の作物を{___}台無しにした。
(The storm completely ruined all of the village's crops.)
explanation: ことごとく (entirely/without exception) — 嵐は作物をことごとく台無しにした。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
私の計画は{___}うまくいかなかった。
(All of my plans failed without exception.)
explanation: ことごとく (entirely) — 私の計画はことごとくうまくいかなかった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女の予想は{___}外れてしまった。
(All of her predictions turned out to be wrong.)
explanation: ことごとく (entirely/all) — 彼女の予想はことごとく外れてしまった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ただ前に進む{___}だ。
(There is nothing to do but move forward.)
explanation: ただ～のみ (nothing but/only) — ただ前に進むのみだ。Expresses single-minded focus or only remaining option.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ただ結果を待つ{___}だった。
(There was nothing to do but wait for the results.)
explanation: ただ～のみ (nothing but) — ただ結果を待つのみだった。Formal/literary expression of sole option.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ただ彼の無事を祈る{___}だ。
(All I can do is pray for his safety.)
explanation: ただ～のみ (nothing but) — ただ祈るのみだ。Formal written style.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その時の私にはただ涙を流す{___}だった。
(At that moment, all I could do was shed tears.)
explanation: ただ～のみ (nothing but) — ただ涙を流すのみだった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電話{___}メール{___}で連絡してください。
(Please contact me by phone or email.)
explanation: なり～なり (either A or B) — 電話なりメールなりで連絡してください。Presents two or more alternatives.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
謝る{___}説明する{___}、何か行動を取るべきだ。
(You should take some action, whether it's apologizing or explaining.)
explanation: なり～なり (either A or B) — 謝るなり説明するなり、行動を取るべきだ。Suggests options available to the listener.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
歩く{___}バスに乗る{___}、早く来てください。
(Whether you walk or take the bus, please come quickly.)
explanation: なり～なり (either A or B) — 歩くなりバスに乗るなり、早く来てください。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
先生{___}先輩{___}に相談してみれば？
(Why not consult with a teacher or a senior colleague?)
explanation: なり～なり (either A or B) — 先生なり先輩なりに相談してみれば？
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
味{___}見た目{___}、この料理は完璧だ。
(Both in flavor and appearance, this dish is perfect.)
explanation: といい～といい (both A and B) — 味といい見た目といい、この料理は完璧だ。Evaluates two aspects as equally noteworthy.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
性格{___}実力{___}、彼は申し分ない候補者だ。
(Both in character and ability, he is an ideal candidate.)
explanation: といい～といい (both A and B) — 性格といい実力といい、申し分ない候補者だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
立地{___}設備{___}、このホテルは最高だ。
(Both the location and the facilities of this hotel are excellent.)
explanation: といい～といい (both A and B) — 立地といい設備といい、このホテルは最高だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
デザイン{___}使いやすさ{___}、この製品はよくできている。
(Both the design and the ease of use of this product are well done.)
explanation: といい～といい (both A and B) — デザインといい使いやすさといい、この製品はよくできている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
平日{___}休日{___}、彼は毎日練習した。
(Whether on weekdays or weekends, he practiced every day.)
explanation: といわず～といわず (whether A or B/throughout) — 平日といわず休日といわず、毎日練習した。Emphasizes no exceptions across contrasting categories.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
昼{___}夜{___}、工事の音が続いた。
(Whether day or night, the construction noise continued.)
explanation: といわず～といわず (whether A or B) — 昼といわず夜といわず、工事の音が続いた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
顔{___}手{___}、泥だらけになって帰ってきた。
(Whether on their face or hands, they came home covered in mud.)
explanation: といわず～といわず (throughout/without exception) — 顔といわず手といわず、泥だらけになった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
春{___}秋{___}、この公園には観光客が絶えない。
(Whether spring or fall, this park never lacks tourists.)
explanation: といわず～といわず (whether A or B) — 春といわず秋といわず、観光客が絶えない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供{___}大人{___}、ルールは守らなければならない。
(Whether child or adult, rules must be followed.)
explanation: であれ～であれ (whether A or B) — 子供であれ大人であれ、ルールは守らなければならない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
成功{___}失敗{___}、挑戦することに意味がある。
(Whether success or failure, there is meaning in trying.)
explanation: であれ～であれ (whether A or B) — 成功であれ失敗であれ、挑戦することに意味がある。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
賛成{___}反対{___}、理由を述べてください。
(Whether in favor or against, please state your reason.)
explanation: であれ～であれ (whether A or B) — 賛成であれ反対であれ、理由を述べてください。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
男性{___}女性{___}、平等に機会が与えられるべきだ。
(Whether male or female, opportunities should be given equally.)
explanation: であれ～であれ (whether A or B) — 男性であれ女性であれ、平等に機会が与えられるべきだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この問題は日本{___}、世界中で議論されている。
(This issue is not only discussed in Japan, but throughout the world.)
explanation: のみならず (not only A but also B) — 日本のみならず、世界中で議論されている。Formal equivalent of だけでなく.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は英語{___}、フランス語も流暢に話す。
(He speaks not only English but also French fluently.)
explanation: のみならず (not only) — 英語のみならず、フランス語も流暢に話す。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その薬は効果がある{___}、副作用も少ない。
(Not only is that medicine effective, but it also has few side effects.)
explanation: のみならず (not only) — 効果があるのみならず、副作用も少ない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この技術は産業{___}、医療分野にも応用されている。
(This technology is applied not only in industry but also in the medical field.)
explanation: のみならず (not only) — 産業のみならず、医療分野にも応用されている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これは命{___}重大な問題だ。
(This is a serious matter related to one's life.)
explanation: にかかわる (related to/affecting) — 命にかかわる重大な問題だ。Often used with weighty subjects like life, honor, reputation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これは会社の名誉{___}スキャンダルだ。
(This is a scandal affecting the company's honor.)
explanation: にかかわる (affecting/related to) — 名誉にかかわるスキャンダルだ。Implies serious consequences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
安全{___}規制は絶対に守らなければならない。
(Safety-related regulations must absolutely be followed.)
explanation: にかかわる (related to) — 安全にかかわる規制は守らなければならない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
それは国家の存亡{___}問題だ。
(That is a matter affecting the survival of the nation.)
explanation: にかかわる (affecting/related to) — 国家の存亡にかかわる問題だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

締め切りを守らないのは彼{___}。
(Not meeting deadlines is not limited to him alone.)
explanation: に限ったことではない (not limited to) — 締め切りを守らないのは彼に限ったことではない。

ストレスを感じるのは社会人{___}。

間違いを犯すのは人間{___}。
(Making mistakes is not something limited to humans.)
explanation: に限ったことではない (not limited to) — 間違いを犯すのは人間に限ったことではない。

孤独を感じるのは一人暮らしの人{___}。
(Feeling lonely is not limited to people living alone.)
explanation: に限ったことではない (not limited to) — 孤独を感じるのは一人暮らしの人に限ったことではない。

quizQuestion:
彼女に謝りた{___}で別れてしまった。
(We parted ways without me ever getting to apologize to her.)
explanation: ずじまい (ended up never doing) — 謝りたいずじまいで別れてしまった. Wait — correct form: 謝ら+ずじまい. Express that intended action never happened.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
結局その本は読ま{___}で返してしまった。
(In the end I returned the book without ever reading it.)
explanation: ずじまい (never ended up doing) — 読まずじまいで返してしまった。Negative stem + ずじまい.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会いたかった祖父に会え{___}だった。
(I never managed to see my grandfather whom I had wanted to visit.)
explanation: ずじまい (never got to do) — 会えずじまいだった。Expresses regret about unfulfilled intention.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
計画していた旅行は実現せ{___}に終わった。
(The trip I had planned ended without ever being realized.)
explanation: ずじまい (never ended up doing) — 実現せずじまいに終わった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
説明せ{___}わかるはずだ。
(You should be able to understand without me explaining.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 説明せずともわかるはずだ。Formal literary expression for 'even without doing'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
言わ{___}、彼女は事情を察してくれた。
(Without me saying anything, she understood the situation.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 言わずとも、事情を察してくれた。Literary/formal register.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
急が{___}、時間はまだある。
(There's no need to rush — there is still time.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 急がずとも、時間はまだある。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
辞書を引か{___}、その単語の意味がわかった。
(I understood the word's meaning without needing to look it up.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 辞書を引かずとも、意味がわかった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今日は良いこと{___}の一日だった。
(Today was a day full of nothing but good things.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely/full of) — 良いことずくめの一日だった。Means 'entirely made up of X'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの人はいつも黒{___}の服装をしている。
(That person always dresses entirely in black.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely/all) — 黒ずくめの服装。Common collocations: 黒ずくめ, 規則ずくめ.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの会社は規則{___}で働きにくいと聞いた。
(I heard that company is all rules and difficult to work at.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely/full of) — 規則ずくめで働きにくい。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この旅行はトラブル{___}で散々だった。
(This trip was terrible — nothing but trouble.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely full of) — トラブルずくめで散々だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は自分の名前{___}書けなかった。
(He couldn't even write his own name.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 自分の名前すら書けなかった。Emphasizes that even the most minimal/expected thing is not achieved.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
忙しくて食事{___}まともに取れていない。
(I'm so busy I can't even eat a proper meal.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 食事すらまともに取れていない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
専門家{___}解けない問題だ。
(It's a problem that even experts cannot solve.)
explanation: ですら (even) — 専門家ですら解けない問題だ。ですら attaches to nouns directly.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一言{___}礼が言えないのか。
(Can't you even say a single word of thanks?)
explanation: すら (even) — 一言すら礼が言えないのか。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんなこと、できる{___}よ。
(There's no way I can do something like that!)
explanation: やしない (emphatic negative) — できやしないよ。Colloquial emphatic negation, strong dismissal.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あいつは絶対に来{___}。
(There's absolutely no way that guy will come.)
explanation: やしない (emphatic negative) — 来やしない。Verb stem + やしない. Colloquial, expresses strong disbelief.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
口では大きいことを言うが、何もし{___}だろう。
(He talks big but won't actually do anything.)
explanation: やしない (emphatic negative) — しやしないだろう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんな難しい試験、受かり{___}よ。
(There's no way I'll pass such a difficult exam.)
explanation: やしない (emphatic negative) — 受かりやしないよ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この問題は{___}解決できない。
(This problem simply cannot be resolved no matter what.)
explanation: どうにも～ない (cannot by any means) — どうにも解決できない。Expresses complete inability despite effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の頑固な態度は{___}変えられない。
(His stubborn attitude simply cannot be changed.)
explanation: どうにも～ない (cannot by any means) — 頑固な態度はどうにも変えられない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この悲しみは{___}癒えない。
(This grief simply cannot be healed.)
explanation: どうにも～ない (cannot by any means) — 悲しみはどうにも癒えない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
締め切りが明日なのに、{___}間に合いそうにない。
(The deadline is tomorrow but it just can't be met no matter what.)
explanation: どうにも～ない (cannot by any means) — どうにも間に合いそうにない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
タイミングを逃して、謝り{___}。
(I missed the moment and failed to apologize.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to do/miss the chance) — 謝りそびれた。Verb stem + そびれる. Regret at missed opportunity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
混んでいてバスに乗り{___}。
(It was crowded and I failed to get on the bus.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to/miss chance) — 乗りそびれた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼が話し続けるので、帰り{___}。
(Because he kept talking, I failed to leave.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to do/miss the chance) — 帰りそびれた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの映画、ずっと見たかったのに見{___}。
(I always wanted to see that movie but failed to watch it.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to do) — 見そびれた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
大事なことを言い{___}。
(I failed to say the important thing.)
explanation: 損なう (fail to do) — 言い損なった。Verb stem + 損なう. Accidentally failed to complete action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
急いでいたので、書類に印鑑を押し{___}。
(Because I was rushing, I failed to stamp the document.)
explanation: 損なう (fail to do) — 押し損なった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電車を乗り{___}ので遅刻した。
(I failed to board the train and was late.)
explanation: 損なう (fail to) — 乗り損なったので遅刻した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
チャンスを生かし{___}のは残念だった。
(It was a shame that I failed to take advantage of the chance.)
explanation: 損なう (fail to) — 生かし損なったのは残念だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もうこうなっては打つ{___}。
(Now that it has come to this, there is nothing we can do.)
explanation: 術がない (no way/means) — 打つ術がない。Formal expression meaning there is no method or means available.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の行方を確かめる{___}状況だった。
(It was a situation where there was no way to confirm his whereabouts.)
explanation: 術がない (no way) — 確かめる術がない状況だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
伝えたくても伝える{___}。
(Even though I want to convey it, there is no way to do so.)
explanation: 術がない (no way/means) — 伝える術がない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この難病を治す{___}のが現状だ。
(The current situation is that there is no way to cure this rare disease.)
explanation: 術がない (no way) — 治す術がないのが現状だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
食事{___}、すぐに仕事に戻った。
(Cutting the meal short, I returned to work immediately.)
explanation: もそこそこに (cutting short/quickly without finishing) — 食事もそこそこに、仕事に戻った。Implies leaving something hastily without completing it properly.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
挨拶{___}、彼は急いで会場を出た。
(Barely finishing his greetings, he rushed out of the venue.)
explanation: もそこそこに (quickly/without finishing properly) — 挨拶もそこそこに、急いで出た。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
返事{___}、彼女は電話を切ってしまった。
(Without barely responding, she hung up the phone.)
explanation: もそこそこに (without properly finishing) — 返事もそこそこに、電話を切った。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
朝食{___}、急いで家を飛び出した。
(Barely touching breakfast, I rushed out of the house.)
explanation: もそこそこに (cutting short) — 朝食もそこそこに、飛び出した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は会社に勤める{___}、小説を書いている。
(While working at a company, he is also writing novels.)
explanation: かたわら (while/alongside) — 会社に勤めるかたわら、小説を書いている。Main activity continues while secondary activity also occurs.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
育児をする{___}、彼女は英語の勉強も続けた。
(While raising children, she also continued studying English.)
explanation: かたわら (while/alongside) — 育児をするかたわら、英語の勉強も続けた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
研究者として働く{___}、大学で教鞭もとっている。
(While working as a researcher, he also teaches at a university.)
explanation: かたわら (while/alongside) — 研究者として働くかたわら、教鞭もとっている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
音楽活動をする{___}、ボランティアにも取り組んでいる。
(While engaging in musical activities, she is also involved in volunteer work.)
explanation: かたわら (while/alongside) — 音楽活動をするかたわら、ボランティアにも取り組んでいる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
波に揺られ{___}揺られ{___}、船は港に近づいた。
(Rocking back and forth with the waves, the ship approached the harbor.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately doing A and B) — 揺られつ揺られつ. Literary pattern showing repetitive alternation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人は追い{___}追われ{___}、駅まで走った。
(The two chased and were chased in turn as they ran to the station.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately) — 追いつ追われつ. Fixed expression meaning chasing back and forth.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
持ち{___}持たれ{___}の関係が大切だ。
(A relationship of mutual support — give and take — is important.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately/mutually) — 持ちつ持たれつの関係。Fixed phrase meaning mutual dependence.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
抜き{___}抜かれ{___}、最後まで接戦が続いた。
(Trading the lead back and forth, it remained a close race to the end.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately) — 抜きつ抜かれつ、接戦が続いた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
絶対に優勝し{___}！
(I will definitely win the championship!)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely do it/show you) — 優勝してみせる！Expresses strong determination to accomplish something.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
諦めずに、必ずやり遂げ{___}。
(Without giving up, I will definitely see it through.)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely do it) — やり遂げてみせる。Shows strong resolve.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
誰が何と言おうと、夢を叶え{___}。
(No matter what anyone says, I will make my dream come true.)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely show them) — 夢を叶えてみせる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一人でこのプロジェクトを完成させ{___}。
(I will complete this project on my own.)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely do it) — 完成させてみせる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
見る{___}テレビを眺めていたら、大切なニュースが流れた。
(While watching TV without really meaning to, an important news story came on.)
explanation: ともなく (unintentionally/without a specific aim) — 見るともなくテレビを眺めていたら。Action done absentmindedly.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
聞く{___}隣の会話が耳に入った。
(Without really meaning to listen, the neighboring conversation reached my ears.)
explanation: ともなく (unintentionally) — 聞くともなく隣の会話が耳に入った。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
どこへ行く{___}、ただ街をぶらぶら歩いた。
(Without any particular destination in mind, I just wandered around town.)
explanation: ともなく (without a specific aim) — どこへ行くともなく、ただぶらぶら歩いた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
誰に言う{___}、ふと口から言葉が出た。
(Without meaning to tell anyone in particular, the words just slipped out.)
explanation: ともなく (without specific intent) — 誰に言うともなく、ふと言葉が出た。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は涙{___}事情を話してくれた。
(She told me the circumstances with tears in her eyes.)
explanation: ながらに (in that state of) — 涙ながらに事情を話してくれた。Describes state maintained throughout an action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
生まれ{___}の才能を活かして活躍している。
(She is active, making use of her innate talent.)
explanation: ながらに (in the very state of) — 生まれながらの才能。Fixed phrase meaning innate/from birth.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
祖父は昔{___}の暮らしを今も続けている。
(My grandfather continues living in the old way even now.)
explanation: ながらに (in that very state of) — 昔ながらの暮らし。Fixed phrase: traditional, unchanged way.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は居{___}にして世界中の情報を得ることができる。
(While staying at home, he can obtain information from all over the world.)
explanation: ながらに (in that state) — 居ながらにして世界中の情報を得ることができる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供は子供{___}考えがある。
(Children have their own way of thinking.)
explanation: なりに/なりの (in one's own way) — 子供は子供なりに考えがある。Appropriate to that person/thing's level or nature.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
私は私{___}精一杯やっている。
(I am doing my very best in my own way.)
explanation: なりに (in one's own way) — 私は私なりに精一杯やっている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
年齢相応{___}落ち着きが出てきた。
(An age-appropriate composure has emerged in him.)
explanation: なりの (appropriate to) — 年齢相応なりの落ち着き。Modifies nouns.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
初心者は初心者{___}楽しみ方がある。
(Beginners have their own way of enjoying things.)
explanation: なりに (in one's own way) — 初心者は初心者なりの楽しみ方がある。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
想像する{___}恐ろしい。
(It is terrifying even to imagine.)
explanation: だに (even/just thinking about it) — 想像するだに恐ろしい。Literary/archaic particle meaning 'even (doing something minimal)'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
考える{___}震えが止まらない。
(I can't stop trembling just thinking about it.)
explanation: だに (even just) — 考えるだに震えが止まらない。Literary emphasis on minimal action triggering strong reaction.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
聞く{___}身の毛がよだつ話だった。
(It was a story that made your hair stand on end just hearing it.)
explanation: だに (even just) — 聞くだに身の毛がよだつ話だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんなことは夢{___}思わなかった。
(I never even dreamed such a thing could happen.)
explanation: だに (even/not even in dreams) — 夢だに思わなかった。Fixed phrase: not even in one's dreams.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
少し{___}力になれれば嬉しいです。
(I would be happy if I could help even a little.)
explanation: なりとも (even a little/at least) — 少しなりとも力になれれば嬉しい。Expresses desire for even a minimal amount.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一言{___}お礼が言いたかった。
(I wanted to say even just a word of thanks.)
explanation: なりとも (even a little/just) — 一言なりともお礼が言いたかった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一時間{___}休みを取りたい。
(I want to take a rest, even if just for one hour.)
explanation: なりとも (even just) — 一時間なりとも休みを取りたい。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
何{___}手伝えることがあれば言ってください。
(Please tell me if there is anything, even something small, I can help with.)
explanation: なりとも (even a little) — 何なりとも手伝えることがあれば言ってください。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
たとえ専門家{___}、この問題は難しい。
(Even experts would find this problem difficult.)
explanation: とて (even/archaic) — たとえ専門家とて、この問題は難しい。Archaic/literary particle meaning 'even'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
王様{___}法を破ることは許されない。
(Even a king is not permitted to break the law.)
explanation: とて (even) — 王様とて法を破ることは許されない。Literary/classical register.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
神様{___}、運命を変えることはできない。
(Even God cannot change fate.)
explanation: とて (even) — 神様とて運命を変えることはできない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
天才{___}、努力なしには成功できない。
(Even a genius cannot succeed without effort.)
explanation: とて (even) — 天才とて、努力なしには成功できない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今度こそ絶対に勝つ{___}！
(This time I will definitely win!)
explanation: ぞ (emphatic sentence-final particle) — 今度こそ絶対に勝つぞ！Strong emphatic assertion, typically masculine speech.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
遅刻したら怒る{___}！
(I'll be angry if you're late!)
explanation: ぞ (emphatic/warning) — 遅刻したら怒るぞ！Used for warnings or strong proclamations.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
俺が守ってやる{___}。
(I'll protect you!)
explanation: ぞ (emphatic) — 俺が守ってやるぞ。Emphatic male speech particle.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
見ていろ、いつか見返してやる{___}。
(Watch me — I'll get back at you someday!)
explanation: ぞ (emphatic determination) — いつか見返してやるぞ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
聞いてる{___}！何度言えばわかるの？
(I'm telling you, I heard it! How many times do I have to say it?)
explanation: ってば (I'm telling you/emphatic) — 聞いてるってば！Expresses frustration that one is not being heard/believed.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もう行くな{___}、なんでついてくるの？
(I told you not to come, so why are you following me?)
explanation: ってば (I'm telling you) — 行くなってば. Expresses repetition of instruction with exasperation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
大丈夫だ{___}、心配しないで。
(I'm telling you I'm fine, don't worry.)
explanation: ってば (I'm telling you) — 大丈夫だってば。Reasserting what one has already said.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
やめて{___}！うるさいよ！
(I'm telling you to stop! You're so loud!)
explanation: ってば (I'm telling you/emphatic demand) — やめてってば！
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
うちの子供{___}、また宿題を忘れてきた。
(That child of mine — he forgot his homework again.)
explanation: ったら (exasperation about someone) — うちの子供ったら、また宿題を忘れてきた。Expresses exasperation about a person's behavior.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの人{___}、いつも遅刻するんだから。
(That person — always being late!)
explanation: ったら (exasperation) — あの人ったら、いつも遅刻するんだから。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もう、あなた{___}、いつもそうなんだから！
(Honestly, you — you're always like this!)
explanation: ったら (exasperation) — あなたったら、いつもそうなんだから！
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
うちの夫{___}、片付けを全くしないんです。
(My husband — he absolutely never tidies up.)
explanation: ったら (exasperation) — うちの夫ったら、片付けを全くしない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供は泣く{___}騒ぐ{___}、大変だった。
(With children crying and making noise, it was overwhelming.)
explanation: わ～わで (overwhelmed by multiple things) — 子供は泣くわ騒ぐわで大変だった。Lists multiple negative/overwhelming events.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電車は遅れる{___}、財布は忘れる{___}、最悪の朝だった。
(With the train being late and forgetting my wallet, it was the worst morning.)
explanation: わ～わで (overwhelmed by multiple issues) — 電車は遅れるわ、財布は忘れるわで、最悪の朝だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
仕事は山積みになる{___}、体調も悪い{___}、もう限界だ。
(With work piling up and feeling unwell too, I'm at my limit.)
explanation: わ～わで (multiple overwhelming things) — 仕事は山積みになるわ、体調も悪いわで、もう限界だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
雨は降る{___}、傘は壊れる{___}、散々な帰り道だった。
(With rain falling and my umbrella breaking, it was a terrible walk home.)
explanation: わ～わで (overwhelmed by multiple events) — 雨は降るわ、傘は壊れるわで、散々だった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
結果{___}、最善を尽くすことが重要だ。
(However the result turns out, giving your best is what matters.)
explanation: はどうであれ (however it turns out/regardless) — 結果はどうであれ、最善を尽くすことが重要だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
動機{___}、行動には責任が伴う。
(Whatever the motive, actions carry responsibility.)
explanation: はどうであれ (however it is) — 動機はどうであれ、行動には責任が伴う。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
理由{___}、約束を破ったことは許せない。
(Whatever the reason, breaking a promise is unforgivable.)
explanation: はどうであれ (whatever it may be) — 理由はどうであれ、約束を破ったことは許せない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
過去{___}、今どう生きるかが大切だ。
(Whatever the past may have been, what matters is how you live now.)
explanation: はどうであれ (however it was) — 過去はどうであれ、今どう生きるかが大切だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
疲れた{___}、お腹が痛い{___}と文句ばかり言っている。
(He keeps complaining — things like 'I'm tired' and 'my stomach hurts'.)
explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints/examples) — 疲れただの、お腹が痛いだのと文句ばかり言っている。Used to list multiple examples, often disparagingly.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
高い{___}、遠い{___}と理由をつけて断った。
(He made excuses like 'it's expensive' and 'it's far away' and declined.)
explanation: だの～だの (listing reasons/examples disparagingly) — 高いだの、遠いだのと理由をつけて断った。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
甘い{___}、辛い{___}と好き嫌いが多い。
(He's so picky, saying things like 'it's too sweet' and 'it's too spicy'.)
explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints) — 甘いだの、辛いだのと好き嫌いが多い。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
眠い{___}、退屈{___}と授業中ずっと不満を言っていた。
(He kept complaining throughout class, saying things like 'I'm sleepy' and 'I'm bored'.)
explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints) — 眠いだの、退屈だのと不満を言っていた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
家族{___}でボランティア活動に参加した。
(The whole family participated together in the volunteer activity.)
explanation: ぐるみ (together with/the whole ~) — 家族ぐるみでボランティア活動に参加した。Means involving the whole group.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会社{___}の不正が発覚した。
(Company-wide fraud was uncovered.)
explanation: ぐるみ (the whole ~/-wide) — 会社ぐるみの不正が発覚した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
街{___}でその映画の撮影に協力した。
(The whole town cooperated in the filming of that movie.)
explanation: ぐるみ (whole ~/-wide) — 街ぐるみで撮影に協力した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
学校{___}で環境保護に取り組んでいる。
(The whole school is working on environmental protection.)
explanation: ぐるみ (whole ~) — 学校ぐるみで環境保護に取り組んでいる。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}ブレーキを踏んで事故を防いだ。
(He stepped on the brakes on the spur of the moment and prevented an accident.)
explanation: とっさに (on the spur of the moment/reflexively) — とっさにブレーキを踏んだ。Instinctive immediate response.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}子供をかばって怪我をしてしまった。
(On the spur of the moment she shielded the child and got injured.)
explanation: とっさに (reflexively/instinctively) — とっさに子供をかばった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}嘘をついてしまったが、後悔している。
(On impulse I told a lie, and I now regret it.)
explanation: とっさに (on the spur of the moment) — とっさに嘘をついてしまった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ボールが飛んできたので、{___}頭を下げた。
(Since the ball came flying, I ducked my head on reflex.)
explanation: とっさに (reflexively) — とっさに頭を下げた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
文句を言い出したら{___}。
(Once you start complaining, there's no end to it.)
explanation: きりがない (there's no end to it/endless) — 文句を言い出したらきりがない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
欲しいものを挙げれば{___}。
(If I start listing things I want, there's no end to it.)
explanation: きりがない (endless) — 欲しいものを挙げればきりがない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
問題点を数え上げたら{___}ので、主なものだけ説明します。
(If I were to list all the problems there would be no end, so I'll explain only the main ones.)
explanation: きりがない (endless) — 問題点を数え上げたらきりがない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の失敗を指摘し始めたら{___}だろう。
(If you start pointing out his mistakes, there will be no end to it.)
explanation: きりがない (endless) — 失敗を指摘し始めたらきりがないだろう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
謝るくらいなら、黙っている{___}。
(Rather than apologizing, I would rather keep quiet.)
explanation: ほうがましだ (would rather/better than) — 黙っているほうがましだ。Choosing the lesser of two undesirable options.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんな仕事をするくらいなら、辞めた{___}。
(If it means doing work like that, I would rather quit.)
explanation: ほうがましだ (would rather) — 辞めたほうがましだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんな人に頼むくらいなら、自分でやった{___}。
(Rather than asking someone like that, I'd rather do it myself.)
explanation: ほうがましだ (would rather) — 自分でやったほうがましだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
恥をかくくらいなら、最初からやらない{___}。
(Rather than being embarrassed, I'd rather not do it from the start.)
explanation: ほうがましだ (would rather) — やらないほうがましだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このビュッフェは食べ{___}です。
(This buffet is all-you-can-eat.)
explanation: 放題 (all one wants/as much as one likes) — 食べ放題。Verb stem + 放題. Unlimited doing of the verb.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供たちは庭で走り{___}遊んだ。
(The children ran and played as much as they wanted in the garden.)
explanation: 放題 (as much as one wants) — 走り放題遊んだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は悪口を言い{___}言っていた。
(He was saying bad things about others without restraint.)
explanation: 放題 (without restraint/as much as one wants) — 悪口を言い放題言っていた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その映画配信サービスは月額料金で見{___}だ。
(That streaming service lets you watch as many movies as you want for a monthly fee.)
explanation: 放題 (all you can/unlimited) — 見放題だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人はただの友達{___}。
(The two of them are not anything special — just friends.)
explanation: でも何でもない (not at all/nothing special) — ただの友達でも何でもない。Denies any special status or meaning.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これは秘密{___}、誰に話しても構わない。
(This isn't a secret at all — you can tell anyone.)
explanation: でも何でもない (not at all) — 秘密でも何でもない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼の行動は特別なこと{___}、普通のことだ。
(His actions are nothing special — just normal behavior.)
explanation: でも何でもない (not at all/nothing special) — 特別なことでも何でもない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これは難しい問題{___}、誰でも解ける。
(This is not a difficult problem at all — anyone can solve it.)
explanation: でも何でもない (not at all) — 難しい問題でも何でもない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼はここに住んでいる{___}、毎日来ている。
(He comes here every day — it's practically the same as if he lived here.)
explanation: も同然だ (same as/as good as) — ここに住んでいるも同然だ。Expresses that something is practically equivalent.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人は家族{___}の仲の良さだ。
(The two are close like family — practically the same as family.)
explanation: も同然だ (same as/as good as) — 家族も同然の仲の良さだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この機械はもう壊れている{___}。
(This machine is practically already broken.)
explanation: も同然だ (same as/as good as) — 壊れているも同然だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
試合に出られないなら負けた{___}。
(If I can't play in the match, it's practically the same as losing.)
explanation: も同然だ (same as/as good as) — 負けたも同然だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
危険{___}、彼は燃える建物に飛び込んだ。
(Disregarding the danger, he plunged into the burning building.)
explanation: も顧みず (disregarding/heedless of) — 危険も顧みず、建物に飛び込んだ。Ignoring risks or concerns.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
体の疲れ{___}、夜を徹して作業を続けた。
(Disregarding physical exhaustion, he worked through the night.)
explanation: も顧みず (disregarding) — 体の疲れも顧みず、夜を徹して作業を続けた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
自分の健康{___}、仕事に打ち込んだ。
(Heedless of his own health, he threw himself into work.)
explanation: も顧みず (disregarding) — 自分の健康も顧みず、仕事に打ち込んだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
家族の反対{___}、彼は海外移住を決めた。
(Disregarding his family's opposition, he decided to move abroad.)
explanation: も顧みず (disregarding) — 家族の反対も顧みず、海外移住を決めた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女はその日、しゃべり{___}だった。
(She talked on and on without stopping all that day.)
explanation: まくる (do repeatedly/without stopping) — しゃべりまくる。Verb stem + まくる. Emphatic continuous action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
試験前夜、問題集を解き{___}た。
(The night before the exam, he kept solving problem after problem without stopping.)
explanation: まくる (repeatedly/intensively) — 問題集を解きまくった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
バーゲンで洋服を買い{___}た。
(She bought clothes at the sale one after another without stopping.)
explanation: まくる (repeatedly) — 洋服を買いまくった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は敵チームを攻め{___}て、大勝利を収めた。
(He attacked the opposing team over and over and achieved a great victory.)
explanation: まくる (repeatedly/intensively) — 攻めまくって大勝利を収めた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
病気の体{___}、式典に出席した。
(Despite being ill, she pushed herself to attend the ceremony.)
explanation: を押して (despite opposition/pushing through) — 病気の体を押して、式典に出席した。Acting despite physical or social resistance.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
反対意見{___}、計画を推進した。
(Pushing through despite opposition, he advanced the plan.)
explanation: を押して (pushing through despite) — 反対意見を押して、計画を推進した。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
医師の忠告{___}、彼はアルコールを飲み続けた。
(Despite the doctor's advice, he continued to drink alcohol.)
explanation: を押して (despite/pushing through) — 医師の忠告を押して、飲み続けた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
疲労{___}、最後まで試合に出続けた。
(Pushing through fatigue, he continued playing in the match to the end.)
explanation: を押して (pushing through despite) — 疲労を押して、試合に出続けた。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
皆が努力している中、彼女{___}まったく練習もしていない。
(While everyone is working hard, when it comes to her, she hasn't even practiced at all.)
explanation: に至っては (as an extreme case) — 彼女に至ってはまったく練習もしていない。Introduces an extreme example, often negative.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
他の社員は残業しているのに、部長{___}定時に帰ってしまう。
(While other employees work overtime, when it comes to the manager, he goes home right on time.)
explanation: に至っては (taking it to the extreme case of) — 部長に至っては定時に帰ってしまう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
多くの国で規制が強化された中、この国{___}全く逆の政策を取っている。
(While many countries have tightened regulations, when it comes to this country, it has taken the completely opposite policy.)
explanation: に至っては (as an extreme case of) — この国に至っては全く逆の政策を取っている。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
若者でさえ運動不足だというのに、高齢者{___}なおさらだ。
(Even young people lack exercise, so when it comes to the elderly, it's all the more so.)
explanation: に至っては (extreme case) — 高齢者に至ってはなおさらだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
うちの上司{___}、いつも人のせいにするんだから。
(When it comes to my boss, he always blames others.)
explanation: ときたら (when it comes to/speaking of with complaint) — 上司ときたら、いつも人のせいにする。Strong complaint about a specific person or thing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの店{___}、サービスが悪い上に値段も高い。
(When it comes to that store, the service is bad and the prices are high on top of that.)
explanation: ときたら (speaking of/with complaint) — あの店ときたら、サービスが悪い上に値段も高い。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
最近の若者{___}、挨拶もまともにできないのが多い。
(When it comes to young people these days, many can't even greet others properly.)
explanation: ときたら (speaking of with criticism) — 最近の若者ときたら、挨拶もまともにできないのが多い。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この天気{___}、雨が降ったり止んだりで本当に嫌だ。
(When it comes to this weather, the rain going on and off is really unpleasant.)
explanation: ときたら (speaking of with complaint) — この天気ときたら、雨が降ったり止んだりで嫌だ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
試験に合格した{___}、次は就職活動だ。
(Now that I've passed the exam, the next thing is job hunting.)
explanation: となると (if that is the case/now that) — 試験に合格したとなると、次は就職活動だ。Draws a consequence from a new situation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会長が辞任した{___}、後任は誰になるのだろう。
(Now that the chairperson has resigned, I wonder who will succeed them.)
explanation: となると (if that is the case) — 会長が辞任したとなると、後任は誰になるのだろう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
雨天{___}、試合は中止になる可能性がある。
(If it comes to rain, the match may be cancelled.)
explanation: となると (if it comes to/now that) — 雨天となると、試合は中止になる可能性がある。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
海外赴任{___}、家族全員で引っ越す必要がある。
(If it comes to an overseas posting, the whole family will need to relocate.)
explanation: となると (if it comes to) — 海外赴任となると、家族全員で引っ越す必要がある。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
天気予報では晴れだと言っていた。{___}、当日は大雨だった。
(The weather forecast said it would be sunny. However, on the day it rained heavily.)
explanation: ところが (however/unexpectedly) — 天気予報では晴れだと言っていた。ところが、当日は大雨だった。Introduces an unexpected contrary result.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
練習を重ねて、優勝できると思っていた。{___}、一回戦で負けてしまった。
(I kept practicing and thought I could win. However, I was knocked out in the first round.)
explanation: ところが (however/unexpectedly) — ところが、一回戦で負けてしまった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
簡単な仕事だと聞いていた。{___}、やってみるとかなり難しかった。
(I heard it was easy work. However, when I tried it was quite difficult.)
explanation: ところが (however/unexpectedly) — ところが、やってみるとかなり難しかった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は必ず来ると言っていた。{___}、約束の時間を過ぎても現れなかった。
(He said he would definitely come. However, he didn't appear even after the agreed time had passed.)
explanation: ところが (however) — ところが、約束の時間を過ぎても現れなかった。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電気が消えている。{___}、誰もいないのだろう。
(The lights are off. That means no one is here, I suppose.)
explanation: ということは (that means/in other words) — 電気が消えている。ということは、誰もいないのだろう。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼が来ないと言った。{___}、計画を変更しなければならない。
(He said he wouldn't come. That means we must change our plans.)
explanation: ということは (that means) — 彼が来ないと言った。ということは、計画を変更しなければならない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今日が締め切りだ。{___}、今すぐ提出しなければならない。
(Today is the deadline. That means I must submit it right away.)
explanation: ということは (that means) — 今日が締め切りだ。ということは、今すぐ提出しなければならない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もう3時だ。{___}、出発まであと1時間しかない。
(It's already 3 o'clock. That means there's only one hour left until departure.)
explanation: ということは (that means) — もう3時だ。ということは、出発まであと1時間しかない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}本番となると、緊張してしまった。
(When it actually came to the performance, I got nervous.)
explanation: いざ (when it comes to/at the critical moment) — いざ本番となると、緊張してしまった。Contrast between expectation and reality at the crucial moment.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
{___}戦いとなれば、誰も彼にはかなわない。
(When it actually comes to fighting, no one can match him.)
explanation: いざ (when it actually comes to) — いざ戦いとなれば、誰も彼にはかなわない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
準備は万端だ。{___}出発だ！
(Preparations are complete. Now, let's depart!)
explanation: いざ (now/at this moment — call to action) — 準備は万端だ。いざ出発だ！Also used as a call to action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
平時は誰でも勇敢に見える。しかし{___}というときに本性が出る。
(Everyone looks brave in peacetime. But at the critical moment, one's true nature emerges.)
explanation: いざ (at the critical moment) — いざというときに本性が出る。Fixed phrase: いざというとき = when push comes to shove.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
こんなところで休んで{___}！早く行こう。
(This is no time to be resting here! Let's go quickly.)
explanation: ている場合ではない (this is no time for) — 休んでいる場合ではない！Urgent rejection of a current action as inappropriate given circumstances.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ているわけにはいかない
ているどころではない
reading: ているばあいではない
pronunciation: te iru baai de wa nai

quizQuestion:
試験が明日なのに、ゲームをし{___}。
(The exam is tomorrow, so this is no time to be playing games.)
explanation: ている場合ではない (no time for) — ゲームをしている場合ではない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ているわけにはいかない
ているどころではない
reading: ているばあいではない
pronunciation: te iru baai de wa nai

quizQuestion:
緊急事態だ。のんびりし{___}。
(It's an emergency. This is no time to be relaxing.)
explanation: ている場合ではない (no time for) — のんびりしている場合ではない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ているわけにはいかない
ているどころではない
reading: ているばあいではない
pronunciation: te iru baai de wa nai

quizQuestion:
締め切りが迫っているのに、迷っ{___}。
(The deadline is approaching, so this is no time to be hesitating.)
explanation: ている場合ではない (no time for) — 迷っている場合ではない。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ているわけにはいかない
ているどころではない
reading: ているばあいではない
pronunciation: te iru baai de wa nai

quizQuestion:
あの時の感動{___}なかった！本当に素晴らしかった。
(The emotion I felt at that moment — there are no words! It was truly wonderful.)
explanation: といったら (speaking of/words can't describe) — あの時の感動といったらなかった！Expresses that something is beyond description.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あのラーメンのおいしさ{___}、毎週通うほどだ。
(Speaking of how delicious that ramen is, I go there every week.)
explanation: といったら (speaking of — beyond description) — おいしさといったら、毎週通うほどだ。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その時の恥ずかしさ{___}ありませんでした。
(The embarrassment I felt at that time was beyond words.)
explanation: といったら (beyond description — set phrase) — 恥ずかしさといったらありませんでした。Fixed pattern: といったら(ない/ありません).
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あの子の素直さ{___}、本当に癒される。
(Speaking of that child's innocence, it is truly heartwarming.)
explanation: といったら (speaking of — expressing strong feeling) — 素直さといったら、本当に癒される。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は自分の名前{___}漢字で書けない。
(He cannot write even his own name in kanji.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 自分の名前すら漢字で書けない。Literary emphasis that the most basic thing is impossible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
忙しすぎて、休む時間{___}ない。
(I am too busy — there is not even time to rest.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 休む時間すらない。Literary: even the minimal thing is absent.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
ベテランの選手{___}解けない問題だ。
(It is a problem that even veteran players cannot solve.)
explanation: ですら (even) — ベテランの選手ですら解けない問題だ。ですら directly follows nouns.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は挨拶{___}せずに部屋を出て行った。
(She left the room without even saying hello.)
explanation: すら (even) — 挨拶すらせずに部屋を出て行った。
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
